action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 3 failed immediately with AttributeError because model.py used torch.avg_pool2d instead of torch.nn.functional.avg_pool2d. The workspace shows model.py, train.py, and predict.py. Iter 1/2 results (~1.5% avg accuracy) were catastrophically low because a torchvision ResNet-18 pretrained on ImageNet was used on 32x32 inputs. Analysis: The custom CIFAR ResNet written in iter 3 is the right architecture, but has the avg_pool2d typo. I must fix model.py, ensure train.py is complete, run a quick overfit sanity check on 128 samples, then run a full 5-epoch-per-task replay baseline to re-establish a working baseline and submit eval. Decision: Rewrite model.py (fix pool typo, use F.relu/avg_pool2d), rewrite train.py to ensure buffer logic is complete, keep predict.py, then run sanity check + full training + submit eval.
action.command:
python -c "
import torch
from model import CIFAR100ResNet
device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
model = CIFAR100ResNet().to(device)
data = torch.load('/data/task_0/train.pt')
images = data['images'][:128].to(device)
labels = data['labels'][:128].to(device)
opt = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)
crit = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
for i in range(50):
    opt.zero_grad()
    out = model(images)
    loss = crit(out, labels)
    loss.backward()
    opt.step()
    if i % 10 == 0:
        acc = out.argmax(1).eq(labels).float().mean().item()
        print(f'sanity step {i} loss {loss.item():.4f} acc {acc:.4f}')
print('Sanity check passed')
" && python train.py --epochs 5 --batch_size 128 --lr 0.1 --memory_per_class 20 --output_dir checkpoints
